public_functions.behavior_description: Initialize the GTM PWM subsystem to generate three complementary, center-aligned PWM pairs at the requested switching frequency. Steps: 1) Enable the GTM module and enable the functional clocks required by the TOM submodule. 2) Create and initialize a TOM timer configuration with defaults, set the desired PWM frequency and select the TOM instance and its base channel (channel 0) as the time base using the specified clock source. Initialize the timer driver with this configuration. 3) Configure the six output pins used by the three complementary pairs via the GTM pin-map API to assign the TOM TOUT functions and to set the pad mode to push-pull with the requested pad driver. 4) Create and initialize a PWMHL (high/low complementary) configuration with defaults: set three channel pairs, assign the previously configured timer handle, select the same TOM instance as the timer, set dead-time and minimum pulse constraints, set both high- and low-side active states, and set the output mode and pad driver to match the requirements. Initialize the PWMHL driver. 5) Select center-aligned PWM mode in the PWMHL driver. 6) Compute initial ON-time ticks for each channel pair from the timer period using the required initial duty fractions (e.g., 25%, 50%, 75%), then perform a coherent update: disable updates on the timer, write the ON-time array to the PWMHL driver, and apply the update to transfer shadow values to active registers. The function stores/uses driver handles so that runtime updates can adjust on-times coherently. Pin mapping verification (TC387 LFBGA-292): The requested pins are P02.0, P02.1, P02.2, P02.4, P02.5, and P02.7. Use IfxGtm_PinMap.h to confirm that each of these pins provides a TOM1 TOUT function and to identify the exact TOM1 channel number and TOUT index for each pin (look for symbols like IfxGtm_TOM1_TOUTxx_P02_y_OUT). If any of the requested P02.* pins on the TC387 LFBGA-292 package do not expose the required TOM1 TOUT signals, user confirmation is required and the TOM instance selection remains TBD; in that case, either choose a TOM instance/channels that match the available TOUTs on these pins or revise the pin selection to match TOM1. Error handling/return: This function returns void and, as documented here, does not perform explicit error checking on the iLLD calls. It assumes the GTM enable/clock and TOM timer/PWMHL initialization calls succeed. For production use, check and handle return values where applicable (e.g., boolean status from IfxGtm_Tom_Timer_init and IfxGtm_Tom_PwmHl_init) and report failures via assertions, logging, or an application-specific error hook.

Perform a periodic duty-cycle ramp on all three PWM channel pairs with wrap-around and apply the new ON-times coherently. Algorithm: 1) Read the current timer period. 2) Compute a duty increment step as a fraction of the period and compute lower/upper ON-time boundaries (minimum and maximum allowed duty) from the same period. 3) For each of the three channels: add the step to the current ON-time. If any updated ON-time reaches or exceeds the maximum boundary, wrap it back to the minimum boundary. 4) Disable timer updates, write the updated ON-time array to the PWMHL driver, and apply the update so that all channels switch to the new ON-times atomically at the next update point. This maintains complementary, center-aligned operation with the configured dead-time and minimum pulse constraints. Error handling/return: This function returns void and does not perform explicit error checks; it assumes valid driver handles and that update/apply operations succeed. For robustness, add range checks on computed ON-times and handle driver-call failures per application policy (e.g., assert, log, or error hook).

header_selection.reconciliation_reason: LLM driver_calls use IfxGtm_Tom_* API, not IfxGtm_Tom_PwmHl (the reference API). Updated to match actual usage.
